def: adj. See s.v. گرو guru. adj. & s.m. (fem. gurvi, or gurviṇī), Heavy, weighty; great, large, extended, long (as a vowel), grave (as an accent); difficult, hard; high (in degree), vehement, violent, excessive, deep, much; important, serious, momentous; valuable, highly prized, dear, beloved; venerable, honourable, respectable; excellent; best; — one who is an object of reverence, a venerable or respectable person (as a father, mother, &c.); a spiritual parent or preceptor (from whom a youth receives the initiatory Mantra or prayer and who conducts the necessary ceremonies up to the period of investiture with the sacrificial thread); a spiritual guide, a religious teacher (one who explains the law and religion to his pupil, instructs him in the Śāstras, &c.); a pastor; a teacher (generally); a superior; — a name of Brihaspati or the planet Jupiter (who is considered as the guru or preceptor of the gods); — a wise man, a sage ( = gur-gyānī); — (ironic.) a consummate knave
subs: vulg. gur-bhāʼī, s.m. Follower of the same spiritual preceptor or guide, fellow-sectarian, fellow-disciple; fellow-student
s.m. (f. -ī), The child (natural or adopted) of a religious preceptor

vulg. gur-jan, s.m. pl. Religious or venerable persons, eminent persons, great people, respectable people; — s.m. sing. A venerable person (as a father, a religious preceptor, &c.), an elder, a superior
vulg. gur-daććhanā, s.f. A fee given to a spiritual preceptor; rent-free land given to a religious teacher
s.m. The pronouncing a blessing on a religious guide
s.m. A respectful epithet of any guru
s.m. The eighth Nakshatra or lunar constellation Pushya (of which Brihaspati is the ruling deity)
s.m. A serious or momentous affair; — the business or office of a spiritual teacher
s.m. 'A guru with bells' (hung round his neck), a thorough knave
vulg. guru-laćh, or gur-laćh, s.m. = next
adj. (f. -ā), Heavy, hard of digestion; — s.m. Knowledge which can be acquired from a living instructor only
s.f. The wife of a spiritual teacher
(or gur-) mukh honā, v.n. To receive the initiatory Mantra from a guru; to become a disciple or pupil
s.f. Name of the character in which the Panjābī language is written
vulg. gur-bār, s.m. 'The day of Jupiter,' Thursday.

def: s.m. = gurutā, q.v. s.f. Weight, heaviness; burden, trouble; weightiness, gravity, importance, dignity; — the position or office of a (religious) teacher, tutorship.
adj. & s.f. Heavy (with child), pregnant; — a pregnant woman; — the wife of a guru or teacher.
adj. & s.f. Heavy (with child), pregnant; — a pregnant woman ( = garbhinī).

ety: Pehl. vazr; Zend vazra, fr. vaz, 'To strengthen,' &c.; S. वज्र, rt. वज्
def: s.m. A mace, a club; an iron club (pointed at one end, and having a knob at the other covered with spikes); — a battle-axe
more: n. m. An iron club; a mace. gurz-bardār , n. m. A mace-bearer. gurz-mār , n. m. A Mah. Faqīr, who carries a club armed with spikes with which he wounds himself to extort alms.
s. m. A mace, a sort of iron club pointed at one end and having a knob at the other covered with spikes; a battle-axe. گرز بردار gurz-bardār, A mace-bearer.
subs: s.m. A (Mohammadan) faqīr who carries a club armed with spikes with which he wounds himself to extort alms.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُرْز (gurz). In the Indo-Persian tradition, it became associated with lightning-bolts, thunder and storms. It became a metaphor for strength and force as well, especially in relation to the warrior and fighter.

def: s.m. A bound, jump.

s.m. 'The master or possessor of cows or of herds'; an epithet of the Deity; — a religious mendicant (a faqīr), a devotee, a saint or holy man; — a caste of Brāhmaṇs, descendants of the disciples of Ćaitanya of Nadiyā; a Brāhmaṇ of this class; — an honorary title affixed to proper names.

ety: g̠uṣṣa, q.v. + Prk. इल्लो and इल्लओ = S. इलः and इल+कः
def: adj. Angry, wrathful, passionate, irascible, choleric, furious, fierce.

n. m. 1. See سائيں Gusāīṅ te gadhā bhalā jo dhobī ghare lādī ḍhove . Bhoj. Prov. More useful than the priest is the laundress' donkey who bears a load to the house. 2. A saint; a holy man; a devotee; priest. Jo ye bachche mere parvān chaṛh jāëṅ to maiṅ jānūṅ, Gusāīṅ jī! raṅḍāpe meṅ bharosā hai Gusaiyāṅ kā . If but this child grow up to wed God's grace I'll own, O priest! a widow I, my trust's in God alone. 3. A caste of Brahmans. 4. A title of respect.

s.m. 'The master or possessor of cows or of herds'; an epithet of the Deity; — a religious mendicant (a faqīr), a devotee, a saint or holy man; — a caste of Brāhmaṇs, descendants of the disciples of Ćaitanya of Nadiyā; a Brāhmaṇ of this class; — an honorary title affixed to proper names.

s.m. = gusāʼīṅ, q.v. s.m. 'The master or possessor of cows or of herds'; an epithet of the Deity; — a religious mendicant (a faqīr), a devotee, a saint or holy man; — a caste of Brāhmaṇs, descendants of the disciples of Ćaitanya of Nadiyā; a Brāhmaṇ of this class; — an honorary title affixed to proper names.

ety: for A. غسالة, v.n. fr. غسل 'to wash'
def: s.m. Water with which a thing has been washed; — bathing.
more: s. m. 1. Dirty water with which any thing has been washed. 2. Bathing.

def: s.m. = next, q.v.

r: gusār, guzār, guẕār, gusārdan, guzārdan, guẕāshtan
ety: i.q. guzār, or guẕār, q.v.; — rt. of gusārdan = guzārdan = guẕāshtan
def: part. adj. Letting go; taking away, removing, dissipating, assuaging; drinking, swallowing, taking down (used as last member of compounds, e.g. g̠am-gusār, mai-gusār, qq.v.).
more: part. act. (from گساردن) in compos. Letting go, taking away, drinking or dissipating (wine, grief).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Passionate, irascible, furious, choleric.
s.f. An assembly, a meeting, a cabal, a council; a society, an association
subs: v.n. A council to be held, &c.

def: s.f. An assembly, a meeting, a cabal, a council; a society, an association
subs: v.n. A council to be held, &c.

ety: rt. of gusistan; — ˚sil, for sal = Zend śar = S. शर्; see gusista
def: part. adj. Breaking, tearing; destroying; removing (used as last member of compounds; e.g. gam-gusil, 'destroying or removing care').

ety: g̠uṣṣa, q.v. + Prk. इल्लो and इल्लओ = S. इलः and इल+कः
def: adj. Angry, wrathful, passionate, irascible, choleric, furious, fierce.
more: A. P. adj. Irascible; choleric; passionate.

r: gusista, gusistan, gusīḵẖtan, gu, vi, sistan, sīḵẖtan, śar
ety: perf. part. of gusistan = gusīḵẖtan; gu = Zend vi = S. वि; sistan or sīḵẖtan, fr. Zend rt. śar = S. शर् (श्टनाति
def: part. adj. Broken; interrupted; abrupt.
s.f. Breaking; breach, fracture; interruption.
s.m. A complete (purificatory) washing of the whole person; bathing, ablution, a bath
more: n. m. 1. Bathing; a bath. 2. ( Mah .) Purifying by frequent immersion in water. gusal-khānā , n. m. A bath-room; bagnio. gusl-i-sehat , n. m. Bathing after recovering from sickness. gusal k. , v. n. To bathe. gusul kī hājat honā , v. n. To be polluted. gusal, yā gusl-i-maiyat denā , v. a. To wash a corpse.
s. m. Bathing, ablution. غسل خانه g̠ẖusl-ḵẖāna, s. m. A bath, bagnio.
subs: (-ko), To wash, bathe; to wash a corpse ( = g̠usl-ě-maiyit denā)
or g̠usl-ě-siḥḥat, s.m. Bathing after recovery from sickness
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غسل (ġusl), from Arabic غُسْل (ḡusl).

ety: for A. غصة, v.n. fr. غصّ 'to be choked,' &c.
def: s.m. Choking, strangulation, suffocation; — (choking) wrath, rage, anger, passion; — grief, disquietude of mind, anxiety; — g̠uṣṣe, adv. In anger; from or through anger
more: n. m. غص his throat choked. Rage ( tezī , 6). 1. Gussā harām hai . Prov. Anger is a bastard (or unlawfull). 2. Gusse meṅ bhalāī burāī nahīṅ sūjhtī . Prov. Anger knows not good from bad. 3. Zarā gussā ā jāë, dekho! kaisā pīṭtā hūṅ! Wait till I am the least bit angry, and see how I'll beat you! 4. Gusse meṅ aql jātī rahtī hai . Anger is an enemy to reason. 5. Zor thoṛā, gussa bahut, mār khāne kī nishānī . Prov. Weak and angry, punishment sure. 6. Inkī nāk par gussā rakkhā hī rahtā hai . Prov. His nose is alwsys red with passion. gussā pīnā, mārnā, yā thūknā , Tir. gussa ghoṅṭnā , v. n. To suppress one's anger. 1. Ham to pī jāte lahū dushman kā, par kuchh sochkar Zafar. Dil meṅ apne gussā apnā, aë sitam-gar, pī gaë . I would have drunk my rival's blood but then I thought a while And in my heart my rage suppressed, O cruel fair one, smile! 2. Chalo, jāne do! gussā thūk do! gussā dilānā , v. …

(from غصّ g̠ẖaṣṣa, To be choked or suffocated) s. m. 1. Strangulation, suffocation. 2. ( met. ) Anxiety, grief. 3. (in Hindūst.) Anger, passion. غصي g̠ẖuṣṣe, In anger. غصي سي بهوت هوجانا g̠ẖuṣṣe se bhūt hojānā, To rage like a fiend. غصه کرنا g̠ẖuṣṣa karnā, To rage, to be in a passion. غصه ناک g̠ẖuṣṣa-nāk, adj. Passionate. غصه ور g̠ẖuṣṣa-war, adj. Passionate, cholerick.
subs: or g̠uṣṣa thūknā or thūk-denā, or g̠uṣṣa khānā or khā-jānā, or g̠uṣṣa mārnā (apnā), 'To gulp down, or spit out, or swallow, (one's) anger,' to suppress anger, restrain or subdue wrath
(-ko), or g̠uṣṣe-meṅ lānā (-ko), To make angry, to enrage, put into a state of anger or wrath, to provoke, to offend
(or g̠uṣṣe-ke māre) bhūt ho-jānā, To become a very devil through rage, to rage like a fiend
(-par), To be angry or in a passion (with), to rage (against)
To be filled with anger or rage, to be or become enraged
v.n. To be easily offended, to be very choleric, or irritable
adj. Angry, wrathful, passionate, choleric, furious, fierce
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غصه (ġussa, “grief, sorrow”), from Arabic غُصّة (ḡuṣṣa, “distress, pang”). Compare Gujarati ગુસ્સો (gusso).

def: s.m. corr. of غصه guṣṣa, q.v. s.m. Choking, strangulation, suffocation; — (choking) wrath, rage, anger, passion; — grief, disquietude of mind, anxiety; — g̠uṣṣe, adv. In anger; from or through anger
subs: or g̠uṣṣa thūknā or thūk-denā, or g̠uṣṣa khānā or khā-jānā, or g̠uṣṣa mārnā (apnā), 'To gulp down, or spit out, or swallow, (one's) anger,' to suppress anger, restrain or subdue wrath
(-ko), or g̠uṣṣe-meṅ lānā (-ko), To make angry, to enrage, put into a state of anger or wrath, to provoke, to offend
(or g̠uṣṣe-ke māre) bhūt ho-jānā, To become a very devil through rage, to rage like a fiend
(-par), To be angry or in a passion (with), to rage (against)
To be filled with anger or rage, to be or become enraged
v.n. To be easily offended, to be very choleric, or irritable
adj. Angry, wrathful, passionate, choleric, furious, fierce

def: adj. Presumptuous, arrogant, insolent, audacious, impudent, saucy, uncivil, rude; cruel; abrupt.
more: adj. Impudent; audacious (بےادب 2). gustākhā'nah , adj. Presumptuously; arrogantly; rudely.
adj. Presumptuous, arrogant, insolent, saucy, uncivil, cruel, rude, abrupt. گستاخانه gustāḵẖ-āna, adv. Presumptuously, arrogantly: adj. see گستاخ.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُسْتاخ.

def: adv. Presumptuously, arrogantly, rudely, &c. (see gustāḵẖ).
s.f. Presumption, arrogance, insolence, audacity, assurance, sauciness, rudeness; contempt (of court); cruelty
more: n. f. See بےادبي 1. Presumption; audacity. 2. Contempt (of court). gustākhī k. , v. a. To be insolent; to presume. gustākhī muāf , intj. Pardon my rudeness!
s. f. Presumption, arrogance, insolence, sauciness, assurance, rudeness, cruelty. گستاخي کرني gustāḵẖī-karnī, To presume, to be arrogant, &c.
subs: To presume, to be arrogant, or insolent, or rude, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُسْتَاخِی (gustāxī). By surface analysis, گُسْتَاخ (gustāx) + ـی (-ī).

ety: rt. of gustardan; — gu = Zend vi = S. वि; ˚stardan, fr. Zend śtar = S. स्तर् (स्तृ)
def: part. adj. & s.m. Spreading, strewing, scattering, diffusing; — spreader, &c.; dispenser; administrator (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ʻadālat-gustar, 'One who diffuses or dispenses justice').
more: part. act. (from گستردن) in compos. Spreading, strewing, scattering, diffusing; spreader, dispenser, administrator.

ety: perf. part. of gustardan; see gustar
def: part. Spread (as a bed, &c.); having spread; — s.m. That which is spread; a foundation.
more: part. pass. (from گستردن) Spread, having spread.

ety: gustar, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Spreading, scattering, strewing; diffusing; dispensing, &c. (used as last member of compounds; e.g. ʻadālat-gustarī, 'the dispensing or administering of justice').
v.t. To swallow, gulp, bolt; — v.n. To coo (as a dove, &c.)
more: v. n. To coo as a dove. 2. To swallow.

def: v. n. To gulp down. Chār khāīṅ chaṭrā kī, chār khāīṅ maṭrā kī, Sorah guṭak gaī gehuan kī! Wom. song. Four cakes of gram he ate, and four of peas, and sixteen wheaten cakes gulped down!
v.n. To coo.
v.t. To thread, string; to entangle; to entwine; to embarrass; to engage in; — to fix; to contrive.
more: v. a. Caus. of گتنا q. v.

def: s.m. = غٿرغون g̠uṭar-g̠ūṅ, q.v.
n. m. Cooing (of pigeons, doves). guṭar-gūṅ k., guṭaknā , v. a. To coo; bill and coo.
s.m. A kind of prickly plant, Solanum melongena (syn. bhaṭ-kaṭaiyā).
n. m. A prickly plant (بهتَکتَيا); solanum melongena , Wat.

ety: Prk. गुत्थं; S. ग्रथितं; or v.n. of gūthnā, q.v.
def: s.m. A round ball (of cotton, &c.); a string or thread (of anything).
more: s. m. A round ball of cotton, &c.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Blunted; set on edge (the teeth).
s.m. A large lump; a large stone (of fruit), &c. ( = guṭṭhal, and guṭhlī).
more: adj. Being set on edge (the teeth).

def: v.n. To be dulled or blunted; to be set on edge (the teeth).

s.f. A knot; a lump, mass (as of curd in milk, or of flour in broth, &c.), a clot (of blood); a ball, lump, gathering (as of cotton in a quilt, &c.); — kernel; stone (of a fruit); — a gland; a glandular swelling; a bump; a hard boil or tumour; hard lump (in a woman's breast); hard part (of a boil, &c.); a lump of hardened fæces.
more: s. f. A kernel, stone (of fruit, &c.), seed.

def: n. f. See āṅṭhī. Ber guṭhlī sab' hazam . Prov. Saying. Pulp and seed both swallowed. (Undiscerning).

ety: gūth˚ = Prk. गुत्थो = S. ग्रथितः, rt. ग्रथ्
def: v.t. To thread, string; to plait, braid; to stitch; to put on a spit or skewer, to spit ( = gūṅthnā).
more: v. a. 1. To string; thread. 2. To plait; braid.
( गुंफन or caus. of گتهنا) v. a. 1. To plait, to thread, to string. 2. To stitch. 3. To spit, or put on a spit.

ety: gut˚ or guth˚ = Prk. गुत्थ(इ) = S. ग्रथ्य(ते), pass. of rt. ग्रथ्
def: v.n. To be strung, to be threaded; to be interlaced, or intertwined, or intermingled; to be braided, be plaited; to be or become tangled (as thread, &c.), to be ravelled; to tangle; to catch, hitch; to be entangled (in); to be embarrassed, be involved in difficulties; — to close (with, -se, an antagonist, in wrestling, syn. juṭnā); — to be taken up (with), be occupied, or engaged (in), be absorbed (in); to be under employment, or in use
more: v. a. S. गुफ arrange, string. 1. To be threaded; strung; braided; plaited. 2. To wrestle or close with ( juṭnā 3). 3. To be taken up with; absorbed. Khiyāl meṅ guthā huā hai .
v. n. To be plaited, to be threaded, to be strung.
subs: (dialec.), v.t. To settle, fix, determine, contrive ( = gutānā).

ety: Prk. गुत्थओ (with w inserted); S. ग्रथित+कः
def: adj. (f. -wīṅ), Strung together; interlaced, intertwined; plaited, &c.
adj. Plaited or strung together. Guthvāṅ ḍorā .
v.t. = guṅthwānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to string, &c.; — to cause to be strung; to have or get plaited, &c. (see gūṅthnā).
s.f. A pill, bolus; a small globe or ball, a bullet, a marble, a pellet; a pearl.

s.m. (dialec.) = guttā, q.v. s.m. (dialec.) Exclusive right (of sale, supply, &c.); monopoly, contract; — an income of variable amount sold (or let) for a fixed sum; lease (of land, &c.), farm (syn. ijāra).
more: n. m. S. गुटिका 1. A magic ball prepared by devotees, by putting which in their mouths they are believed to become invisible. 2. A small book worn as an amulet. 3. A hand book; a manual. 4. A sweetmeat swallowed as a bolus without chewing. Chāṭ hai dahī ke guṭke kī! Delhī street cry. 5. A folded betel leaf with catechu, lime, etc. 6. Shards used in play.

def: s.m. A small ball, a bolus; a magic ball prepared by devotees (which, if put into the mouth, is supposed to make a person invisible, or to accomplish something wonderful); — a sweetmeat swallowed as a bolus without chewing; — a small book worn as an amulet; — a little book (bound or boarded); a handbook, a manual; — a flake of pān or betel-leaf (syn. khīlī); a mouthful; a gulp; — a round pebble, a shard (used in play; cf. giṭkarī, and gittī).
more: s. m. 1. A ball prepared by devotees, by putting which in their mouths they are supposed to become invisible. 2. A sweetmeat swallowed as a bolus without chewing. 3. A small book worn as an amulet.

ety: guṭkā or giṭkā, q.v. + S. इनी
def: adj. & s.f. = guṭṭī, fem. of guṭṭā, q.v.
( Mech .) Cor. of dog chuck.
s.m. = H گٿلي गुटली guṭlī, s.f. dialec. forms of guṭhlā, and guṭhlī, qq.v.
s.f. = H گٿلا गुटला guṭlā, s.m. dialec. forms of guṭhlā, and guṭhlī, qq.v.
more: s. (dakh.) Stone (of fruit, Harris ): see گٿهلي guṭhlī.

ety: gut˚ or guth˚ = Prk. गुत्थ(इ) = S. ग्रथ्य(ते), pass. of rt. ग्रथ्
def: v.n. To be strung, to be threaded; to be interlaced, or intertwined, or intermingled; to be braided, be plaited; to be or become tangled (as thread, &c.), to be ravelled; to tangle; to catch, hitch; to be entangled (in); to be embarrassed, be involved in difficulties; — to close (with, -se, an antagonist, in wrestling, syn. juṭnā); — to be taken up (with), be occupied, or engaged (in), be absorbed (in); to be under employment, or in use
more: 1. v. n. (dakh. i. q. مشغول هونا) To be occupied or busied (?). 2. v. a. (i. q. مقرر کرنا) To settle, to fix, to establish (?).
subs: (dialec.), v.t. To settle, fix, determine, contrive ( = gutānā).

def: s.m. (dialec.) Exclusive right (of sale, supply, &c.); monopoly, contract; — an income of variable amount sold (or let) for a fixed sum; lease (of land, &c.), farm (syn. ijāra).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Short of stature, stumpy, stunted; — a stumpy person, a dwarf.

ety: prob. Prk. गुत्थलो or गुट्ठलो(?); S. ग्रथ्+अलः
def: adj. & s.m. Having knots, knotted; lumpy, bumpy; coagulated; hardened; having a large stone (as a mango, &c.); — a knot; a hard lump; a large bump; coagulation; — a gland; a glandular swelling; a hard boil or tumour (cf. guṭhlī).
n. m. 1. Coagulation ( āṅṭhī , 1). 2. A hard lump; a knot. Razaī meṅ guṭṭhal paṛ gaë . 3. A gland ( gānṭh ). guṭ'ṭhal , adj. Having a large stone (a fruit). Guṭṭhal ām .
s.f. Betel-nut, the nut of Areca catechu.
s.m. A witness, an evidence; testimony, proof
more: n. m. pl. گواﮩـان A witness. gavāh banānā, yā ṭhairānā , v. a. To name a person as a witness. gavāh-i-tālīmī, sikhāyā yā paṛhāyā gavāh , A tutored witness. gavāh-i-hāshiyah, yā mundarjā dastāvez , A marginal witness; an attesting witness. gavāh denā , v. a. To produce a witness. gavāh-i-rūyat, gavāh-i-chashm-dīd, dinī, yā mashāhdā , An eye-witness ( sākshī , 3). gavāh-i-samāī , n. m. A hearsay witness. gavāh-i-aqd-i-nikāh , n. A witness to a marriage contract. gavāh k. , v. a. To make one a witness. gavāh ko paṛhānā, yā sikhānā . To instruct a witness. gavāh muddaī , A witness for the prosecution. gavāh-i-muddāilah , n. m. A witness for the defence. gavāhoṅ kī ism-navīsī . A list of witnesses.
s. m. A witness, an evidence.
subs: or gawāh ṭhaharānā (-ko), To make (one) a witness, to name (a person) as a witness
or gawāh karnā (-ko), To bring forward (or to produce) evidence or a witness or witnesses; to make (one) a witness
(-ko), To bring evidence; to take a witness; to invoke
(-par), To become a witness; to bear testimony (to).
wety: From Classical Persian گواه (guwāh).

def: s.f. Evidence, witness, testimony; deposition; written testimony
more: s. f. Evidence, testimony, witness. گواهي دينا gawāhī denā, To witness, testify.
subs: (-par), To give evidence, to witness (to), bear testimony (to), testify, attest; to depose (to)
or gawāhī likhnā (-par), To witness (a document), to attest.

ety: P. go+waiyā, as in the preceding
def: adj. Conversible; eloquent; — s.f. A gossip; a (woman's) female friend, &c. ( = guʼiyāṅ, q.v.).
more: s. f. (gossip) A womanʼs female friend. adj. Eloquent, conversible.

def: s.m. The betel-nut tree, Areca faufel or catechu.

ety: rt. of guwāshtan, or guwārīdan; gu = Zend vi = S. वि+wār, fr. Pehl. var = Zend var = S. वर् (वृ)

r: hā, hāʼe-hawwaz, h, hawwaz, hāʼe-mudawwara, ha, h., hāʼe-jalī, hāʼe-z̤āhir, hāʼe-malfūz̤ī, bādshāh, hāʼe-mukhtafī, hāʼe-maktūbī, bahāna, ha-kār
ety: called hāʼe-hawwaz, 'h of the word hawwaz,' or hāʼe-mudawwara, 'the round h'
def: s.m. The letter or sound ha.
more: called hā-i-havvaz , and hāë mudavvarah , the 27th letter of the Arabic and 31st of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to ह in Sanskrit. It has the sound of the English h . When it occurs at the end of a word, it is either perceptible, hā-i-zāhir or— jalī , or— malfūzī , as in bādshāh a king; or imperceptible ( makhfī or— maktūbī ), as in parvāna a writ, ba with, etc. where it merely conveys the sound of fatha . When it occurs at the end of Arabic words it is generally with two points over it, thus ة and is pronounced as t . In Abjad it stands for five, and in almanacs it may represent Thursday or Virgo. It is interchangeable as an initial, medial, and final with s , q. v. as hai=sai is. As an initial and medial with g , as W. he = E ge ho! hap=gap gulp; H. hans = Eng. goose; H. harā = Eng. green; hī = Sant. gī very; sahodrā= sagodrā of the same womb: with b , as hai =Bhoj. bā is; Eng. hu…

the twenty-seventh letter of the Arabic and thirty-first of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to ह in Sanskrit. It is one of the guttural letters, having the sound of the English h. When it occurs at the end of a word, it is either perceptible, which is called هاي ظاهريا جلي يا ملفوظي ( hā-i-z̤āhir or -jalī, or -malfūz̤ī ), as in بادشاه bādshāh, A king; or imperceptible مختفي يا مکتوبي ( -muḵẖtafī or -maktūbī ), as in بہانه bahāna, A pretence, به ba, With, &c. where it merely conveys the sound of fatʼḥa. In the formation of the plural with ها the هاي جلي of the singular is preserved; as راهہا rāh-hā, Roads; اندوه ها andoh-hā, Griefs, &c. whereas the هاي مختفي disappears; as, جامہا jāma-hā, Robes. بندها banda-hā, Slaves, &c. wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حبشه (habša), from Arabic الحَبْشَة (al-ḥabša).

def: n. prop. The people or race of Abyssinians.
adj. & s.m. Of, or belonging to, or relating to, Abyssinia or the Abyssinians; Abyssinian; negro; black; — an Abyssinian or Ethiopian; a Caffre; a negro; a black slave.
more: n. m. habshan , n. f. 1. An African; Abyssinian; Ethiopian; kaffre; negro. 2. A black slave. habshī, habshan , adj. As black as a negro; black. Habshī halvā-sohan .
(belonging to حبش) adj. Abyssinian, a Caffre, negro.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حبشی (habašī, “Abyssinian; Negro; black slave”), from Arabic حَبَشِيّ (ḥabašiyy).

def: s.f. A grain (of corn, &c.); seed, kernel, or stone (of fruit); a berry; — a pill
more: n. A grain; berry; a pill.
s. f. 1. A berry, grain of corn, fruit, &c. 2. (usually in Hind.) A pill. (Pl. حبوب ḥubūb ).
subs: s.f. The fruit of the tree called 'balm of Gilead,' Amyris gileadensis
wety: From Arabic حَبّ (ḥabb).

def: E. n. m. (Nurses) Infantile bronchitis and pleurisy; chincough ( masān , 3).

ety: for A. حبّة ḥabbat, fem.; n. of un. fr. ḥabb, q.v.
def: s.m. One grain, one berry, one seed (syn. dāna); a pill; — a grain (weight = the weight of a grain of barley); a measure ( = two barley-corns); a particle, a small piece or portion (of), a jot or tittle.
more: n. m. 1. A grain; berry; pill; kaurī. Habbā habbā vusūl pāyā . 2. A jot; tittle. habbā bhar , adj. Very little.
s. m. 1. One berry, one grain, one seed. 2. A particle, a part. 3. A measure equal to two barley-corns.

ety: habbā, prob. = S. हम्बा or हम्भा 'making a lowing sound,' + ḍabbā, 'the chest,' q.v.
def: s.m. Bronchitis; pleurisy

ety: ḥabba, perf. of rt. حب 'to love'+zā
def: intj. Charming, excellent, bravo!

old H. forms = hāv, q.v. s.m. Calling; call, cry; — (prob. fr. bhāva), any feminine or coquettish act or gesture, tending to excite amorous sensations; coquetry, blandishment, the airs and arts of lovers
subs: or hāʼo-bhāʼo, s.m. Actions and postures expressive of sentiment or amorous passion; — blandishments, airs, &c. (i.q. hāv); charm, attraction; gesticulation
s.f. Call, cry; cries; — demand, want (e.g. ḵẖarć-kī hāʼo-hāʼo rahtī hai).

def: s.m. corr. of havi, q.v. part. & s.m. Collecting; comprising, comprehending, including; getting within one's grasp; — a collector; a comprehender, &c.
s.m. A person loved or beloved; a lover, sweetheart; a friend.
more: n. m. A lover; sweetheart; mistress; friend; a beloved. Peshā habībullah, jo na kare so lānat-ullāh . Prov. God loves those who work, the idle are accursed.
(from حبّ) adj. used substantively, Beloved, a lover, sweetheart, a friend, a mistress.

def: prop. n. Abel (i.q. hābīl).
s.m. A rope, cord; a vein; bond, cause of union; a covenant, compact, league, alliance
more: (Heb. ) s. m. 1. Rope, cord. 2. A league, alliance, compact. حبل الوريد ḥablu-l-warīd, Jugular vein.
subs: s.m. One of the carotid arteries, the jugular vein.

ety: prob. S. स्तब्ध+ṛā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Ill-shaped; clumsy, awkward; — repulsive, forbidding.
s.m. Confinement, imprisonment, restriction, retention, restraint, keeping in; — a place of confinement, a prison or jail; — a prisoner; — a dam, mound, bank; a reservoir, tank, pond
more: n. m. حبس he confined it. 1. Imprisonment; confinement; a prison. 2. Suffocation; stifling; choking. habs-ul-baul , n. m. Retention of urine; strangury. habs-ul-nafs . Suffocation. habs-i-bejā , G. G. Wrongful confinement; unlawful imprisonment. habs-i-dam ; Pop. habs-dam , n. m. Retaining the breath, which some faqīrs do as a religious act for a very long time. This is esteemed a means of prolonging life, for since the number of inspirations for every man is foredoomed the slower the inspirations the longer will be the period of his existence. habs-i-davām ba-abūr-i-daryā-i-shor . G. G. Transportation for life beyond seas.

(Heb. ) s. m. 1. Retention, imprisonment. 2. Prison. 3. A prisoner. 4. A bank, mound, dam. 5. A tank, pond, reservoir. حبس البول ḥabsu-l-baul, s. m. A retention of urine, a strangury. حبس النفس ḥabsu-n-nafas, Suffocation. حبس دم ḥabs-i-dam, s. m. 1. Retaining the breath (which some faḳirs do as a religious act for a very long time. This is esteemed a means of prolonging life, on the principle, that every man has a predestined number of inspirations to make, and the more slowly these are performed, the longer will be the period of his existence). 2. An asthma.
subs: s.m. Retention of urine, a strangury

s.m. Keeping in the breath (which some faqīrs do as a religious act for a very long time; this is esteemed a means of prolonging life, on the principle that every man has a predestined number of inspirations to make, and the more slowly these are performed, the longer will be the period of his existence); — an asthma
Transportation for life beyond the sea

r: ḥabshin, ḥabshan, ḥabshinī, ḥabshanī, ḥabashī, inī
def: s.f. A female Abyssinian; — ḥabshan, adj. = ḥabshī, q.v.

r: ḥabshinī, ḥabshanī, ḥabshin, ḥabshan, ḥabashī, inī
def: s.f. A female Abyssinian; — ḥabshan, adj. = ḥabshī, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of هبط 'to let down, throw down; to lower (a price
def: s.m. Falling, fall, misfortune; — decrease, fall, reduction (of price, &c.); attenuation, emaciation.
Brij. 1. A freebooter; highwayman. 2. A bugaboo (to frighten children). Bahut shokhī na karo, hābūṛā pakaṛ le-jāegā!
s.m. (dialec.) A freebooter; highwayman; — a bugaboo (to frighten children.
n. m. Jolt; shake ( dhachkā 1).
s. m. (dakh.) Bone. هڐ پهوٿنا haḍ phūṭnā (met. for ترقي پانا) To improve, to advance (?).
s.m. A mark, butt (for archers, &c.)
more: s. m. (dakh. s. f.) A mark, a butt or aim (for archers, &c.). هدف مارنا hadaf mārnā, To strike the mark, to hit the nail on the head.
subs: To strike the mark; to hit the nail on the head.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic هَدَف (hadaf).

ety: contrac. of hadarāhaṭ = hadar(nā) + āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. (dialec.) Shaking tremulously, trembling, quaking; — earthquake.

ety: hadar˚, prob. = Prk. हदड = S. हत (p.p.p. rt. हन्)+र
def: v.n. (dialec.) To shake tremulously (as from a shock or concussion), to tremble, shiver, quake; to vibrate; — to be jolted.
more: v. n. (dakh.) To quake, shiver, tremble ( Harris ).

def: s.m. Anything existing for the first time, recently happening, a thing not known before, a new thing, a novelty, an innovation; an accident, event, casualty, contingency; — an accidental cause of impurity (according to Musalmān traditions) that forbids or prevents one's performing prayer, &c. without ablution; a state annulling legal purity.
more: s. m. 1. Any thing existing for the first time, recently happening, novelty, contingency, event, accident. 2. An accidental cause of impurity, according to Musalmān traditions, whereby it becomes unlawful to commence or finish a prayer without ablution.

def: s.m. pl. (of hadīya, q.v.), Gifts, presents.
s.m. Breaking, demolishing, ruining; — adj. Liberal, generous.
more: 1. s. m. Breaking, demolishing, ruining. 2. adj. Liberal.

def: adj. (f. ḥādda), Sharp, pointed, piercing; acute; pungent, bitter, acrid; sour; ardent, fiery, caustic, poignant (syn. tez)
more: (from حدت which from حدّ Heb. Was sharp or swift) adj. Acrid, sharp, bitter, sour, ardent, fiery, poignant, caustick.

def: s.f. Boundary, term, limit, bounds, extreme, extremity, extent; — bar, obstruction, impediment, separation; — a definition; — the point, or verge (of an event), utmost point or degree; — a starting-post; — a restrictive ordinance or statute (of God, respecting things lawful or unlawful); castigations or punishments appointed to be inflicted (agreeably to the laws of Muhammad) for certain crimes; — adv. At most; at least; extremely, very ( = ḥad-se; az ḥad; e.g. ḥad burā maʻlūm hotā hai)
more: n. f. حد was conterminous with. 1. Boundary; limit; term. 2. A landmark; bar (of a harbour). 3. The utmost point or degree; the maximum ( ant , 1). Had ho gaī . It is the ne plus ultra . 4. A definition. had , adv. At most; at least. Chhai mahīne, had baras roz . For six months, at most a year. had bāṅdhnā, had bandī k., had bast k., had muqarrar k. , v. a. To fix, determine, or mark off the limits or boundaries; to define. had-bast , n. f. The adjustment, settlement, or demarcation of boundaries. had-bast arāzī , n. A landmark. hadd-i-bulūg/?/ , n. f. Majority; full age. hadd-i-bulūg/?/ ko pahauṅchnā , v. n. To attain puberty or majority. had-bandī kī takrār , Disputed boundaries; a boundary case. had-pār, had-bāhar , An outlaw. had toṛnā , v. a. To pass or transgress bounds; to encroach. had javāb , Rejoinder; surrejoinder. hadd-i-samāٴat , n. Limitation statute. hadd-i-samāat se bāhar…

s. f. 1. Boundary, limit, bounds, extreme, extremity. 2. An impediment. 3. A definition. 4. A starting-post. 5. Punishment agreeable to the laws of Muhammad, which fix the exact number of lashes, &c. to be inflicted for certain crimes. Also, same as ازحد az ḥadd or حدسي ḥadd se or حدسي افزون ḥadd se afzūn or حدسي زياد ḥadd se ziyād or حد سي زياده ḥadd se ziyāda, Beyond bounds, extremely. حد باندهنا ḥadd bāndhnā, To bound, to terminate. حد بهر کرنا ḥadd bhar karnā, To do oneʼs best. حد کرنا ḥadd karnā, To push any thing to extremity, to go beyond bounds. حد مارنا ḥadd mārnā, To punish.
subs: (-kā or -ke), To fix, or determine, or mark the limits or boundaries (of), to bound, terminate; to define
ḥad-bandī, s.f. Fixing the limits or boundaries (of); — ḥad-bast karnā, or ḥad-bandī karnā (-kī) = ḥad bāṅdhnā
ḥad-se bāhar, ḥad-se ziyāda, adv. = ḥad-se, q.v.
v.n. To overstep bounds, go beyond limits, to exceed one's power; to transgress; to encroach (on)
s.f. Encroachment, violation of a (neighbour's) landmark; trespass
v.n. To push to extremity, to go beyond bounds; to reach the utmost limits
'Within defined limits,' a term in leases or farming contracts which recognizes the power of the farmer over all the lands and crops within the defined limits
adj. & adv. Without limit, unboundless, endless, infinite; immoderate, excessive, unfathomable; — immoderately, excessively, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حد (hadd), from Arabic حَدّ (ḥadd).

def: A red wasp.
s.m. A wasp.
more: s. m. Bone; spavin. هڐي موتهري نکالنا haḍḍe mothre nikālnā, 1. ( lit. ) To break out in spavins and œdematous swellings. 2. ( met. ) To adopt an evil conduct.

ety: S. हड्ड+क; — हड्ड is prob. a Prk. form of S. (Ved.) अस्थ = S. अस्थिः
def: s.m. Bone; — an osselet, or ossicle; a spavin
subs: (-ke), To break the bones (of); — to beat severely
(-ke), To break out in spavins and œdematous swellings; — (met.) to take to evil courses

more: adj. Contracted. hādda-uz-zāvīa ; H. sukṛā konā , An acute angle.
subs: sě-ḥadda, adj. & s.m. Having three boundaries; — a place or point where three boundaries meet.

def: (for A. حادة ḥāddat), adj. fem. of ḥādd, q.v.
s.m. A blacksmith.

n. f. haḍ'ḍā , n. m. S. हड्ड Pr. aṭṭhī . 1. A bone. 2. A spavin. 1. Ūpar haḍḍī, bhītar heṛā, Muṅh meṅ gor chalāvat pherā . A Riddle. Ans. Bīj seed. 2. Māṅs sab koī khātā hai, haḍḍā gale meṅ koī nahīṅ bāṅdhtā . Prov. The meat all eat, the bones none wear about their necks. (All sin in secret, none in public). 3. Tanak sī haḍḍī, saṛak se baḍḍī! Riddle. Ans. Thread. haḍḍī bolnā , v. n. To crack (one's bones). haḍ'ḍyāṅ, yā haḍḍe guḍḍe toṛ'nā , v. a. To break one's bones; beat severely. haḍḍī-ṭūṭnā ( Surg .) A fracture. haḍḍī kā joṛ khul jānā , Opening out (a joint). haḍḍe mothre nikalnā . 1. To break out in spavins and œdematous swellings. 2. To take to evil courses. haḍḍiyāṅ nikal ānā , v. n. To be reduced to a mere skeleton.
s.f. Bone; — a spavin; — the hard part in the centre of a carrot or other similar root
more: s. f. 1. A bone. 2. The hard part in the centre of a carrot or other similar root.
subs: v.n. To become like bone, to ossify
(-kī), To break the bones (of); — to beat severely
(-kī), The bones to stick out; to be reduced to a skeleton.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀳𑀟𑁆𑀟 (haḍḍa) (see there for more) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-), or directly borrowed from Sanskrit हड्ड (haḍḍa). By surface analysis, ہَڈّا (haḍḍā) + ـی (-ī). Cognate with Punjabi ہَڈّی (haḍḍī).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Full of bones; bony.
s.f. An osselet, ossicle; a spavin.
s.f. Ossification.

def: v.n. To ossify.
n. m. ﮩدي directing. 1. A guide; director; leader. 2. A spiritual guide; a head of a religious order.
s.f. Cattle carried to Mecca to be sacrificed; — giving as an offering; — a captive; — a bride conveyed to her husband's home; — anything honourable, venerable, or precious.

ety: for A. هادٍ hādiṉ, act. part. of هَدَي 'to guide, direct'
def: s.m. A guide, director, leader; — a spiritual guide; head of a religious order.
more: (part. act. of هديٰ Heb. ) s. m. A director, leader, guide (spiritual).

def: s.m. Iron; an instrument or implement of iron; a helmet.
more: (from حدّ Heb. Was sharp or it sharpened) s. m. Iron.

def: adj. = ḥāẕiq, q.v. adj. & s.m. Sharp, acute, intelligent, penetrating, skilled, skilful, thoroughly learned or versed or proficient (in), ingenious; excelling; — a proficient, an adept, &c.
more: (from حدق) adj. Ingenious, skilful, industrious, adept.

ety: for A. حديقة ḥadīqat, fem.; fr. حدق 'to look at'
def: s.m. A walled garden, a garden, an orchard.
adj. Bony.
adj. New, recent, just appearing, just begun
more: (from حدث Heb. Was new, existed for the first time) adj. New, just appearing. حادث هونا ḥādis̤ honā, To appear, to emerge, to happen, to occur.
subs: v.n. To appear, to emerge; to happen, occur.

def: s.m.f. Narration, narrative, story, history, tradition; a tradition or narration relating to or describing a saying or an action of Mohammad
more: n. f. حدث narrated. The traditional sayings and doings of Mahomed, having for the most part the force of law; the Talmud of the Mahomedans. hadīs vazaī , Opp. of hadīs sahīh . A spurious Hadīs. haddīs kheṅchnā , Mah. Wom. v. n. To abjure. Buā, tum ne to mere ghar āne kī haddīs kheṅch lī . Wom.
(from حدث) s. m. f. 1. A history, a tradition. 2. The traditional sayings of Muhammad. حديث کرنا ḥadīs̤ karnā, To forswear, to abjure, to renounce.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَدِیث (hadīs), from Arabic حَدِيث (ḥadīṯ).

ety: for A. حادثة ḥādis̤at, v.n. fr. حدث
def: s.m. A new thing, a novelty; an accident, incident, event, occurrence, adventure, casualty; a mishap, misfortune, disaster, calamity, affliction.
more: n. m. حدث it was new. A sad event; calamity; misfortune; accident.
s. m. A novelty, incident, accident, occurrence, event, adventure, calamity, misfortune.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَادِثَه (hādisa), from Arabic حَادِثَة (ḥādiṯa).

ety: for A. هدية hadīyat, v.n. fr. هدي 'to direct,' &c.

more: s. m. 1. A present (particularly, to superiours). 2. The price of a ḳurʼān.
subs: (-kā), To make an offering, or a present
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هَدِیَه (hadiya), from Arabic هَدِيَّة (hadiyya). Compare Bengali হাদিয়া (hadiẏa).

def: n. m. 1. A present to a superior. 3. The price of a Koran. hadiyah k. , v. a. To sell a Koran.
adj. (f. -ī), Apprehensive, timid; hesitant, scrupulous; sheepish, shy, coy, bashful.
more: adj. Sheepish, shamefaced, timid, bashful, scrupulous.

def: s.f. Apprehension; timidity; a shrinking (from), hesitation; scrupulousness; sheepishness, shyness, bashfulness.
more: (from هديانا) s. f. Scrupulousness, sheepishness, shiness.

ety: perhaps fr. Prk. हद = S. हत, rt. हन्; or fr. Prk. हद्धि = S. हा+धिक्
def: v.n. To be agitated (about), to be alarmed or apprehensive (of, -se), to be timid or shy (of); to tremble (at), to shrink (from), to hesitate, to scruple (cf. hadarnā).
more: v. n. 1. To hesitate. 2. To be alarmed or apprehensive, to shrink.

def: s.m. Ruin, destruction, demolition, laying waste; — hadim (v.n. fr. هدم), s.m. Destroyer, &c.
more: s. m. 1. Ruin, destruction. 2. A destroyer.

def: v.t. To cause to shake or tremble (by a shock or blow, &c.); to jolt; — to shake, to agitate.
s.m. (Dakh.) A kind of ring for the thumb.
more: s. (dakh.) A kind of ring for the thumb ( Qanoon-e-islam ): see انگشتان angushtān.

def: n. f. A sigh; a deep sigh. 1. Kisī kī hāë na lo! Take not upon thyself the sin of making others sigh! 2. Dunyā duraṅgī, makārā sarāë, Kahīṅ khair khūbī kahīṅ hāë hāë . This world two faces shows, a halting-place where cheats impose, Here merry as a bell all goes, there sighs and tearful woes. hāë k., yā mārnā, hāë kā nārā mārnā , v. n. To heave a deep sigh. Jā ko ḍanḍā tā ko gāë, mat karo koī hāë hāë! Prov. Who wields the stick will have the cow, in vain you sigh and make a row. hāë hāë, hāë daiyā, hāë gazab, hāë qismat, hāë re , intj. Alas ( āh ).

ety: prob. fr. Prk. हा = S. हा; — A. also hāʼe
def: intj. Ah! alas! oh! — s.f. A sigh
more: interj. Alas! ah! s. f. A sigh. هاي مارنا hāʼe mārnā, To sigh.
subs: or hāʼe mārnā, or hāʼe-kā naʻra mārnā, 'To utter the exclamation hāʼe or 'alas!'; to sigh; to lament
intj. Alas, alas! — s.f. Sighs; sighing
To exclaim 'alas, alas!'; — to heave a deep sigh (or deep sighs).

def: intj. S. हा Alas! alas the day! well-a-day ( āh, afsos ), 1. Makhmal kī thaili meṅ haë haë ke bīj . Riddle. Ans. Lāl mirch . Chilli. In a velvet bag the seeds of wailing dwell. 2. Chhor chalī ghar bār, rī yānoṅ kī jamṛī, haë haë! Sāṅpā . A dirge. Your house and home you have left, O mother of your infant child, alas! alas!
adj. Active; alert; — clever.
adj. contrac. of haft-dahum, q.v.

ety: act. part. n. fr. حفظ 'to keep, guard, &c.
def: s.m. A keeper, guardian, protector, preserver, governor; the Preserver of all things (a name of the Deity); — one who has the whole Qǒrʼān by heart; a blind man (so called because such very commonly know the Qǒrʼān by heart); — adj. Having a good memory
more: n. m. حفظ he preserved it. 1. A guardian; keeper; preserver. Lo buā, Khudā hāfiz! Mah. Wom. Well, sister, God protect you! 2. One who has the Quran by heart, a work of religious merit. 3. A blind man, so called because the task of committing the Quran to memory is usually undertaken by blind men as a livelihood. 1. Āṅkhoṅ se sukhī, nām hāfiz jī! Prov. Blessed with eyes, yet Hafiz called! 2. Yoh to āchhā saë, tauṅ is ne hāpaj kahe saë? Rus. Why he has got his eyes, and you call him a ' Hāfiz'? 3. Andhe hāfiz, kāne nawāb . Prov. The blind a Hāfiz, the one-eyed a Nawāb. (The devout man grows blind with getting the Qurān by heart, the eyes of the Chief regard only flatterers and parasites). hāfiz-i-haqīqī , The true God; God.
(from حفظ) s. m. 1. A guardian, governour, preserver. 2. Having a good memory. 3. One who has the whole ḳurʼān by heart. 4. The poetical name of a celebrated poet of Persia (Muhammad Shamsuddīn).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حافظ (hāfiz), from Arabic حَافِظ (ḥāfiẓ), from the verb حَفِظَ (ḥafiẓa, “to preserve, to protect, to defend”).

ety: for A. حافظة ḥāfiz̤at, fem. of حافظ, and = quwwat-ě-ḥāfiz̤a
def: s.m. The retentive faculty of the mind, memory.
more: n. m. Memory; the retentive faculty (quvvat-i-hāfizā) .
s. m. Memory, the retentive faculty. (Called also قوت حافظه).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَافِظَه (hāfiza), from Arabic حَافِظَة (ḥāfiẓa).

def: s.f. Retentiveness of memory; good memory.
more: s. f. Retention of memory, good memory.

def: s.m. Company (of men), congregation, assembly, council.
more: s. m. An assembly, council, congregation.

def: adj. S. sapt , A. sabٴa , Lat. sept , Gr. hept , Seven. haft iqlīm, haft kishvar , n. f. The seven regions; the world. haft-andām , n. f. The great vein that runs through the arm. haft-zabāṅ , n. f. A master of seven languages; a linguist. haft-qalam . 1. Seven sorts of Persian handwriting. 2.
